---
created: "2026-03-22T00:00:00Z"
id: task-sv0c-lexer
key: sv0c-lexer
state: done
tags:
    - sv0
    - sv0c
    - lexer
    - tokenizer
title: "sv0c lexer (tokenizer)"
type: task
---

# sv0c lexer (tokenizer)

## purpose

implement the sv0 lexer in SML/NJ. the lexer transforms UTF-8 source text
into a stream of tokens, each carrying a source span for error reporting.

the lexer is the first pass in the compiler pipeline and must handle:
- all sv0 keywords (let, mut, fn, struct, enum, trait, impl, requires,
  ensures, loop_invariant, match, if, else, while, for, in, return,
  type, newtype, where, pub, module, use, unsafe, move, as, self, Self,
  forall, exists, borrows, no_alias, const, static, true, false,
  Some, None, Ok, Err)
- identifiers (alphanumeric + underscore, cannot start with digit)
- numeric literals (decimal with underscores, hex 0x, binary 0b, octal 0o,
  float with optional type suffix _f32/_f64)
- string literals (double-quoted, with escape sequences)
- operators and punctuation (all operators from the reference table)
- doc comments (/// line comments)
- regular comments (// line comments, /* block comments */)
- whitespace (spaces, tabs, newlines -- significant only for line tracking)

## dependencies

/require: sml

## configuration

/env: SV0C_ROOT=sv0c
/env: SRC_DIR=sv0c/sml
/env: SV0DOC_ROOT=sv0doc

## implementation

### step 1: token datatype

/ai: implement the Token datatype in SML/NJ for sv0c.

     create src/lexer/token.sml defining:

     datatype token =
       (* keywords *)
       LET | MUT | FN | RETURN | IF | ELSE | MATCH | WHILE | FOR | IN
     | STRUCT | ENUM | TRAIT | IMPL | TYPE | NEWTYPE | WHERE
     | PUB | PUB_CRATE | MODULE | USE
     | UNSAFE | MOVE | AS | SELF_LOWER | SELF_UPPER
     | REQUIRES | ENSURES | LOOP_INVARIANT
     | FORALL | EXISTS | BORROWS | NO_ALIAS
     | CONST | STATIC
     | TRUE | FALSE | SOME | NONE | OK | ERR
       (* literals *)
     | INT_LIT of IntInf.int
     | FLOAT_LIT of real
     | STRING_LIT of string
     | CHAR_LIT of char
       (* identifiers *)
     | IDENT of string
       (* operators and punctuation *)
     | PLUS | MINUS | STAR | SLASH | PERCENT
     | EQ_EQ | BANG_EQ | LT | GT | LT_EQ | GT_EQ
     | AMP_AMP | PIPE_PIPE | BANG
     | AMP | PIPE | CARET | TILDE
     | LT_LT | GT_GT
     | EQ | ARROW | FAT_ARROW | DOT_DOT | DOT_DOT_EQ | QUESTION
     | LPAREN | RPAREN | LBRACE | RBRACE | LBRACKET | RBRACKET
     | COMMA | SEMICOLON | COLON | COLON_COLON | DOT
     | HASH_LBRACKET
       (* special *)
     | DOC_COMMENT of string
     | EOF

     type located_token = {token: token, span: Span.span}

     include a tokenToString function for debugging.

     reference: sv0doc/keywords/reference.md

/run: 01-define-tokens.sh

### step 2: lexer implementation

/ai: implement the Lexer structure in SML/NJ for sv0c.

     create src/lexer/lexer.sml with:

     signature LEXER = sig
       type state
       val init : string -> string -> state  (* filename -> source -> state *)
       val next : state -> located_token * state
       val tokenize : string -> string -> located_token list
     end

     structure Lexer :> LEXER = struct
       (* implementation *)
     end

     the lexer should:
     - be a hand-written scanner (no lexer generator)
     - track line and column numbers for span construction
     - handle all numeric literal formats (decimal, hex, binary, octal, float)
     - handle escape sequences in strings (\n, \t, \\, \", \0, \xNN)
     - handle underscore separators in numeric literals (1_000_000)
     - handle type-suffixed literals (3.14_f64)
     - distinguish between keywords and identifiers via lookup table
     - produce meaningful error messages for malformed tokens
     - support error recovery (skip to next valid token start)

/run: 02-build-lexer.sh

### step 3: lexer tests

/ai: write a comprehensive test suite for the sv0c lexer in SML/NJ.

     create sv0c/test/lexer_test.sml with tests for:
     - all keywords tokenize correctly
     - identifiers vs keywords
     - all numeric literal formats
     - string literals with escape sequences
     - all operators and punctuation
     - doc comments
     - error cases (unterminated string, invalid escape, malformed number)
     - source spans are correct (line, column)
     - empty input produces EOF
     - whitespace handling

     use a simple assertion-based test framework:
     fun assertEqual (expected, actual, msg) = ...

/run: 03-test-lexer.sh

## validation

/assert: test -f $SRC_DIR/lexer/token.sml
/assert: test -f $SRC_DIR/lexer/lexer.sml

/ai: review the lexer implementation for correctness and completeness.
     verify that every keyword in sv0doc/keywords/reference.md is handled.
     check that all code examples from the design document would tokenize
     correctly.
